title: oc_concepts
description: Observation Concept instance model schema: defines the atomic unit of clinical data (Observation) with FHIR R5 value types, recursive data definitions, shared dimensions, and observation subtypes. Validates OC vocabulary files.

version.description: Semantic version of the OC concept model

Observation.description: The central Observation entity: a single collected fact about a subject. The atomic unit of clinical data collection.

instanceStructure.description: Named property groups of the observation (Identification, Result, CollectionMethod, etc.), keyed by group name

sharedDimensions.description: Contextual dimensions shared across observations (Subject, Study, Visit, Timing, Treatment, Population, Site)

valueTypeUsageByLayer.description: How different CRAFT framework layers use the value type vocabulary

Cardinality.description: Multiplicity of a relationship or property
Cardinality.title: Cardinality

reference.description: URL to the FHIR data types specification

PrimitiveType.description: Definition of a FHIR R5 primitive data type

ComplexTypeComponent.description: A component within a FHIR complex type (e.g., Quantity.value, CodeableConcept.coding)

ComplexType.description: Definition of a FHIR R5 complex data type (Quantity, CodeableConcept, Identifier)

components.description: Named components of this complex type

compatiblePrimitives.description: Primitive types compatible with this complex type

ValueTypes.description: FHIR-aligned value type vocabulary containing both primitive and complex types

primitiveTypes.description: FHIR R5 primitive types keyed by type name

complexTypes.description: FHIR R5 complex types keyed by type name

QualifierSpec.description: Qualifier metadata on a dimension or data definition — defines the qualifier type and its allowed values (from CDDM_Shared_QualifierTypes)

type.description: Qualifier type name (e.g. IntentType, DerivationStatus, ReferenceFrame)

values.description: Map of qualifier value name to its human-readable description

DataDefinition.description: Recursive building block for observation properties. Each data definition describes a single property with its value type, cardinality, and named relationship. May contain nested dataDefinitions for composite properties (e.g., NormalRange → Low/High, GenomicContext → Gene/Variant/Chromosome).

definition.description: Human-readable definition of this data definition

valueType.description: Value type name: FHIR complex type (Quantity, CodeableConcept, Identifier), primitive type (integer, string, boolean, dateTime), or union (Quantity | CodeableConcept)

cardinality.description: How many instances of this property are allowed

relationship.description: Named relationship this definition represents (e.g. hasTopic, hasValue, codedBy)

qualifiers.description: Qualifier specification if applicable (from CDDM shared types)

dataDefinitions.description: Nested child data definitions (recursive). Enables composite properties like Identification → Topic → DictionaryCoding → PreferredTerm.

ObservationDimension.description: A shared contextual dimension on the Observation (e.g., Subject, Study, Visit, Timing, Treatment, Population, Site). Defines the context in which an observation exists.

valueType.description: Value type for this dimension (FHIR type)

cardinality.description: How many instances of this dimension apply

qualifiers.description: Qualifier specification (e.g. IntentType on Treatment, DerivationStatus on Visit)

dataDefinitions.description: Nested data definitions within this dimension (e.g. Timing → StartDate, EndDate, Duration, StudyDay)

ObservationCategory.description: An observation subtype (EventObservation, InterventionObservation, FindingObservation, FindingAboutObservation). Inherits from Observation and may add domain-specific properties.

definition.description: Human-readable definition of this observation subtype

additionalDefinitions.description: Subtype-specific data definitions (e.g., EventObservation adds Severity, Causality, Outcome; InterventionObservation adds Dose, Route)

ValueTypeUsageExample.description: Example of how a CRAFT framework layer uses the value type vocabulary

level.description: Which value type level this layer primarily uses (primitiveTypes or complexTypes)

examples.description: Map of concept path to value type usage example
